text.title: Multi-Agent Failure Modes
text.summary: Six antipatterns that recur in shipped multi-agent systems, paired with detector + fix.

antipattern.description: Role overlap — two agents share significant nouns in their `role` field and produce duplicated work.
antipattern.reason: Overlap is the dominant first-week bug. Detect: validator script flags shared noun count ≥3 between any pair. Fix: split the overlapping bit into a third agent or absorb it into one of the two; never keep both.

antipattern.description: Bidirectional dependency / deadlock — agent A waits for B and B waits for A; the run hangs until the wall-clock cap.
antipattern.reason: Detect: plan-time cycle check on the agent DAG. Fix: refactor to a DAG. If the dependency feels mutual, you really want a shared workspace (collaborative pattern), not point-to-point messaging.

antipattern.description: No named decision authority — when two agents output conflicting answers, the synthesis step deadlocks or picks arbitrarily.
antipattern.reason: Detect: `decision_authority` field absent or empty in spec. Fix: declare orchestrator / judge / majority / human as the canonical tie-breaker; orchestrator is the default and works for ~80% of cases.

antipattern.description: Context loss on handoff — orchestrator passes only a delta or summary; downstream agent fills the gap with hallucination.
antipattern.reason: Detect: eval reveals downstream agent referencing facts no upstream agent produced. Fix: every handoff message carries the full prior context the downstream needs; let the LLM provider's prefix caching pay for the redundancy.

antipattern.description: Rate-limit amplification — N parallel agents share one API key; effective request rate is N×, key trips its limit, all agents stall.
antipattern.reason: Detect: 429s on a parallel-pattern run that single-agent runs do not trigger. Fix: per-agent token bucket or per-agent API key (the cleaner fix); document the per-key concurrency cap in the spec.

antipattern.description: Echo chamber in debate — both sides converge on the same wrong answer because their underlying models are trained on the same data.
antipattern.reason: Detect: debate runs where proponent and opponent agree by round 2. Fix: assign different model families to the two sides (Anthropic vs OpenAI), or feed the opponent a contrarian system-prompt directive ("you must surface the strongest counter even if you privately agree").
